differences.title: 1. DATA MODEL
differences.leftDesc: Supports document, key-value, and relational views. Versatile querying.
differences.rightDesc: Primarily stores BSON documents. Flexible schema.

differences.title: 2. QUERYING
differences.leftDesc: SQL-like N1QL for complex queries, plus key-based access.
differences.rightDesc: Rich, document-based query language for flexible data retrieval.

differences.title: 3. SCALABILITY
differences.leftDesc: Excellent performance for both scale-up and scale-out architectures.
differences.rightDesc: Designed for horizontal scaling across many servers.

differences.title: 4. CONSISTENCY
differences.leftDesc: Offers configurable consistency levels for read/write operations.
differences.rightDesc: Prioritizes availability and partition tolerance, typically eventual consistency.

differences.title: 5. USE CASES
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for diverse data types and complex applications.
differences.rightDesc: Great for applications needing quick iteration and flexible data.

differences.title: 6. MEMORY MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Optimized for memory-first operations, offering high performance.
differences.rightDesc: Primarily stores data on disk, with memory caching.

differences.title: 7. DISTRIBUTED TRANSACTIONS
differences.leftDesc: Supports ACID transactions across multiple buckets and documents.
differences.rightDesc: ACID guarantees typically apply to single documents.
